div1.entryFree:
Soundly, 1) well, satisfactorily: effect this business s. R3 III, 1, 186.
2) fast, closely, so as not to be easily awakened: to sleep s. Ven. 786. Shr. Ind. 1, 33. H5 IV, 1, 285. Caes. II, 1, 4. when Duncan is asleep, whereto the rather shall his day's hard journey s. invite him, Mcb. I, 7, 63.
3) truly, heartily: if you will love me s. with your French heart, H5 V, 2, 105. suspects, yet s. loves, Oth. III, 3, 170 (Qq strongly).
4) stoutly, strongly, smartly: he shall pay for him, and that s. Tp. II, 2, 81. this will shake your shaking, and that s. Tp. II, 2, 81 let them be hunted s. IV, 263. I had swinged him s. Meas. V, 130. knock me here s. Shr. I, 2, 8. Shr. I, 2, 8 Shr. I, 2, 8 V, 2, 104. Tw. III, 4, 428. H4B V, 4, 21. H5 IV, 7, 136. H6A I, 3, 48. Cor. II, 1, 139. Rom. III, 1, 113. IV, 5, 113. Ant. III, 13, 132.
